title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: DATA FLOW DIAGRAMS (DFDs) & PROCESS MAPPING Intelligent Academic Support Dashboard Extending the IUIU ERP(with NLP Note Translation to Student Mother Tongue) Prepared on: 05 March 2026 1. System Summary (Scope of These DFDs) These DFDs and process maps describe how to implement an Intelligent Academic Support Dashboard that extends the IUIU ERP.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: ese DFDs and process maps describe how to implement an Intelligent Academic Support Dashboard that extends the IUIU ERP. The dashboard provides virtual lecturer rooms, resource management, personalised study planning, progress tracking, AI quiz generation, a course-aware chatbot, and an NLP translation component that converts notes (and optionally quiz/chat outputs) into the international student’s mother tongue based on nationality. 2. Legend (DFD Notation Used in This Document)

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: uts) into the international student’s mother tongue based on nationality. 2. Legend (DFD Notation Used in This Document) External Entity: User/Systems outside dashboard boundary (Student, Lecturer, Admin, ERP, AI Provider).Process: A function that transforms inputs into outputs (P1, P2...).Data Store: Repository/database/storage (D1, D2...).Data Flow: Information moving between entities, processes, and stores. 3. DFD Level 0 (Context Diagram) The context diagram treats the whole dashboard as one system.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: s, processes, and stores. 3. DFD Level 0 (Context Diagram) The context diagram treats the whole dashboard as one system. It exchanges data with Students, Lecturers, Admins, the IUIU ERP, and AI/NLP services. Data stores are listed first, followed by processes and flows. 4.1 Data Stores (Level 1) ID Data Store What It Contains D1 User & Roles Store Dashboard user accounts mapped to ERP IDs; roles (student/lecturer/admin). D2 Course & Enrolment Cache Local cache/index of ERP course units, year/semester, student registrations (read-only mirror). D3

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: rse & Enrolment Cache Local cache/index of ERP course units, year/semester, student registrations (read-only mirror). D3 Resource Repository Files (PDF/DOCX/PPT/Images) + metadata (course unit, topic, uploader, version). D4 Study Plan & Timetable Store Generated schedules, student preferences, plan versions, completion logs. D5 Quiz Bank & Attempts Store Generated quizzes, question metadata, answer keys, student attempts, scores. D6 Chatbot Knowledge Base / Index

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: pts Store Generated quizzes, question metadata, answer keys, student attempts, scores. D6 Chatbot Knowledge Base / Index Vector index or searchable store built from course resources; citations/anchors. D7 Feedback & Notes Store Structured notes/questions per topic/resource; lecturer responses/updates. D8 Translation Glossary & Language Profiles Student nationality → language; course-specific glossary; translated outputs cache. D9 Audit & Metrics Logs Access logs, performance metrics, model usage stats, error logs.

D9 Audit & Metrics Logs Access logs, performance metrics, model usage stats, error logs. 4.2 Processes (Level 1) Process ID Process Name Purpose P1 Authentication & Role-Based Access Validate ERP credentials / SSO; map ERP identity to dashboard roles; create session. P2 ERP Data Sync / Retrieval Pull student/lecturer/course/enrolment data from ERP; update D2. P3 Virtual Lecturer Rooms & Resource Management

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: trieval Pull student/lecturer/course/enrolment data from ERP; update D2. P3 Virtual Lecturer Rooms & Resource Management Create room views by year/semester/course unit; upload, tag, version resources; store in D3. P4 Student Resource Access & Search Show resources based on enrolment; filter/search; track access events in D9. P5 AI Study Timetable & Adaptive Planner Collect preferences; generate plan; adjust plan using progress/quiz data; store in D4. P6 Progress Tracking & Analytics

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: Collect preferences; generate plan; adjust plan using progress/quiz data; store in D4. P6 Progress Tracking & Analytics Compute engagement, weak topics, completion; expose dashboards for student/lecturer; write D4/D9. P7 AI Quiz Generation & Delivery Extract text; generate questions; publish quizzes; mark attempts; store in D5. P8 Course-Aware Chatbot (RAG/Knowledge Grounding) Index resources; answer student queries with citations; log interactions; store in D6/D9. P9 NLP Translation to Student Mother Tongue

text: es; answer student queries with citations; log interactions; store in D6/D9. P9 NLP Translation to Student Mother Tongue Detect target language from nationality; translate notes/quiz/chat outputs; store caches/glossary in D8. P10 Student Feedback to Lecturer Submit structured notes; aggregate by topic; notify lecturer; store in D7. P11 Admin Configuration & Moderation Programme mapping, languages, content policy checks, user support, audit review. 4.3 Level 1 Data Flows (High-Level)

P2 ↔ ERP: student/lecturer/course/enrolment; P2 → D2: cached ERP data. Lecturer → P3: resource upload/metadata; P3 → D3: files & metadata; P3 → D6: indexing trigger.

Student → P5: study preferences; P5 → Student: timetable/study plan; P5 ↔ D4: plan versions & completion. P6 ↔ D4/D5/D9: compute analytics; P6 → Student/Lecturer: progress dashboards. Process Mapping (End-to-End and Per-Process Maps) Process mapping shows the sequence of activities and decision points. The maps below are written in a format you can convert into swimlane diagrams (Student / Lecturer / Dashboard / ERP / AI Services).

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: low are written in a format you can convert into swimlane diagrams (Student / Lecturer / Dashboard / ERP / AI Services). 6.1 End-to-End Project Development Process Map (From Idea → Working Prototype) A. Initiation & Planning Define stakeholders (students, lecturers, ERP admin). Confirm scope: faculty/programmes to pilot, semester, course units. Define success metrics (resource access time reduction, quiz usefulness, chatbot satisfaction). Set up project repository, issue tracker, and communication plan. B.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: reduction, quiz usefulness, chatbot satisfaction). Set up project repository, issue tracker, and communication plan. B. Requirements & Analysis Elicit functional requirements (rooms, uploads, planner, quizzes, chatbot, translation). Elicit non-functional requirements (security, availability, performance, privacy). Analyse ERP integration points: APIs, database views, authentication/SSO. Create user stories + acceptance criteria. C. System Design Architecture: web app + AI services; decide monolith vs microservices.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: r stories + acceptance criteria. C. System Design Architecture: web app + AI services; decide monolith vs microservices. Database schema (D1–D9). UI/UX wireframes for student, lecturer, admin. Define DFDs, ERD, sequence diagrams. Define translation strategy (nationality→language mapping; glossary; caching). D. Implementation (Core Platform) Build authentication/authorization (P1). Implement ERP sync/cache module (P2). Implement virtual rooms + resource repository (P3). Implement student browsing/search (P4). E.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: P sync/cache module (P2). Implement virtual rooms + resource repository (P3). Implement student browsing/search (P4). E. Implementation (AI Features) Timetable + planner (P5) and progress tracking (P6). Quiz generation pipeline (P7). Course-aware chatbot with indexing (P8). Translation service integration + glossary (P9). F. Testing & Evaluation Unit tests, integration tests, security tests. Usability testing with pilot users. Evaluate AI outputs (accuracy/relevance), bias, and error handling.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: security tests. Usability testing with pilot users. Evaluate AI outputs (accuracy/relevance), bias, and error handling. Measure performance (latency of quiz/chat/translation). G. Deployment & Documentation Deploy prototype (staging → pilot). Create user guides and admin handbook. Prepare final report, demos, and presentation. Collect feedback and propose next iteration. 6.2 Operational Process Maps (Swimlane-Ready) Each map is presented as: Trigger → Steps → Decision points → Outputs.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: on. 6.2 Operational Process Maps (Swimlane-Ready) Each map is presented as: Trigger → Steps → Decision points → Outputs. Map 1: Student Onboarding & Login (P1 + P2) Student: Open dashboard URL/app. Enter Student No. / email and password (and OTP if enabled). Select current semester (if prompted). Dashboard: Validate inputs; send authentication request to ERP/SSO. On success: create session; load role permissions; create local user profile if first login.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: ication request to ERP/SSO. On success: create session; load role permissions; create local user profile if first login. Fetch cached enrolments (D2); if cache stale, request P2 refresh in background or on-demand. ERP: Return authentication status and basic profile (name, nationality, programme, year). Return enrolment list and course units for current semester (or allow dashboard to fetch via API). Output: Student lands on dashboard with course units, rooms, study tools visible based on enrolment.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: to fetch via API). Output: Student lands on dashboard with course units, rooms, study tools visible based on enrolment. Map 2: Lecturer Uploads Notes to Virtual Room (P3) Lecturer: Login and open a course unit room. Click Upload, choose file(s), enter metadata (topic, week, tags). Submit upload. Dashboard: Validate lecturer-course mapping and file type/size. Store file and metadata; create version and visibility settings. Trigger indexing for chatbot/quiz extraction. AI Services (Indexing):

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: metadata; create version and visibility settings. Trigger indexing for chatbot/quiz extraction. AI Services (Indexing): Extract text, chunk content, create embeddings, and update knowledge index. Return indexing status. Output: Resource becomes available to enrolled students; appears in search; ready for quiz/chat. Map 3: Student Generates a Study Timetable (P5) Student: Open Study Planner. Enter preferences (available hours, preferred times, days off). Choose goal (revision, catch-up, exam prep) and submit.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: Enter preferences (available hours, preferred times, days off). Choose goal (revision, catch-up, exam prep) and submit. Dashboard: Retrieve course load and calendar constraints. Generate a draft schedule (rule-based or AI-assisted). Store timetable and show editable view. Allow student to accept or adjust; save final plan. AI Service (optional): Propose time allocation per course based on load/weights/weaknesses. Return recommended distribution. Output: Weekly timetable linked to resources; reminders optional.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: d/weights/weaknesses. Return recommended distribution. Output: Weekly timetable linked to resources; reminders optional. Map 4: Quiz Generation and Attempt (P7) Student/Lecturer: Select course unit and topic; choose quiz type and number of questions. Click Generate Quiz (or lecturer schedules a quiz). Student answers questions and submits. Dashboard: Extract relevant text from selected resources. Send generation request to AI; receive quiz items.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: nd submits. Dashboard: Extract relevant text from selected resources. Send generation request to AI; receive quiz items. Store quiz; deliver to student; accept attempt; auto-mark MCQs; save score. AI Service: Generate questions and answers based on content; include explanations. Optionally provide difficulty tags and key concepts. Output: Score + feedback; weak topics stored for planner to adapt. Map 5: Course-Aware Chatbot Q&A (P8) Student: Open chatbot within a specific course unit.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: s stored for planner to adapt. Map 5: Course-Aware Chatbot Q&A (P8) Student: Open chatbot within a specific course unit. Ask a question; optionally choose language (auto). Dashboard: Retrieve relevant chunks from knowledge base. Send query + context to AI; receive answer with citations. Display answer; log interaction; allow rating (helpful/not). AI Service: Generate response grounded in retrieved notes; avoid hallucinations; include citations. Output:

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: elpful/not). AI Service: Generate response grounded in retrieved notes; avoid hallucinations; include citations. Output: Instant explanation; common questions aggregated for lecturer analytics. Map 6: NLP Translation to Mother Tongue (P9) Student: Request translation while viewing notes/quiz/chat response. Optionally override language if multilingual. Dashboard: Detect student nationality and map to language profile. Apply glossary constraints for technical terms.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: gual. Dashboard: Detect student nationality and map to language profile. Apply glossary constraints for technical terms. Send translation request; receive translated content. Show bilingual toggle; cache translations. AI/NLP Translation Service: Translate text; preserve meaning, headings, lists, and key medical/technical terms. Return translated output. Output: Translated notes in mother tongue; improved comprehension for international students. Map 7: Student Feedback to Lecturer (P10) Student:

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: in mother tongue; improved comprehension for international students. Map 7: Student Feedback to Lecturer (P10) Student: Under a topic/resource, click ‘I didn’t understand’. Choose difficulty category and write note; submit. Dashboard: Store feedback linked to topic/resource. Aggregate feedback counts by topic; notify lecturer. Lecturer: Review aggregated difficult topics. Post clarification or update resources; mark feedback addressed. Output: More responsive teaching; feedback loop visible in dashboard. 7.

Non-Functional Flows to Include in Your Final Year Project These are often required in system analysis sections and can be included as DFD notes or process constraints: Security & Privacy Role-based access control: students only access enrolled course units. Encrypt sensitive data in transit (HTTPS) and at rest (DB/file storage). Audit trails for logins, uploads, admin changes (D9).

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: t sensitive data in transit (HTTPS) and at rest (DB/file storage). Audit trails for logins, uploads, admin changes (D9). Data minimisation: cache only what’s necessary from ERP (D2). Reliability & Availability Graceful degradation if AI service is down (use rule-based planner, show error & retry for quiz/chat/translation). Background queues for indexing and translation caching. Backups for D3 resources and D4/D5 records. Quality of AI Outputs

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: ackground queues for indexing and translation caching. Backups for D3 resources and D4/D5 records. Quality of AI Outputs Human-in-the-loop: allow lecturers to review quizzes before publishing (optional). Guardrails against hallucinations in chatbot: cite sources; refuse when no evidence. Translation glossary: preserve technical terms; allow lecturer-approved glossary updates. Performance Cache frequently accessed resources; use CDN if needed.

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: cal terms; allow lecturer-approved glossary updates. Performance Cache frequently accessed resources; use CDN if needed. Set latency targets: chat/translation response time; quiz generation acceptable delay. Paginate lists and search results. 8. Quick Checklist (What to Draw in Visio/Draw.io from This Document) DFD Level 0 Context Diagram (entities: Student, Lecturer, Admin, ERP, AI Services; process P0). DFD Level 1 (processes P1–P11 + stores D1–D9).

title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: agram (entities: Student, Lecturer, Admin, ERP, AI Services; process P0). DFD Level 1 (processes P1–P11 + stores D1–D9). DFD Level 2 for each: P1–P11 (you can start by drawing the table steps as arrows). Swimlane process maps: Maps 1–7 (Student / Lecturer / Dashboard / ERP / AI). 9. Gantt Chart (Suggested 6-Week MVP Implementation Plan) Gantt chart showing a realistic 6-week solo development timeline for an MVP prototype. Bars indicate planned work periods, with overlapping tasks where necessary.

text: o development timeline for an MVP prototype. Bars indicate planned work periods, with overlapping tasks where necessary. title: Week 3 ERP Notes
text: ntication, ERP link, and database setup Student █ █ Core platform (virtual rooms, uploads, student access) Student █ █ █ Indexing + AI MVP (planner, quiz, chatbot, translation) Student █ █ █ Feedback/admin + testing + bug fixing Student █ █ Pilot, documentation, report polishing, final demo Student █ Notes:• Use this critical-path chart if you need a simpler supervision timeline for six weeks.• Keep non-essential enhancements optional so the final demo remains stable and complete.

title: Lecture 1
text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. CSC101 Enterprise Systems lecture one introduces enterprise resource planning. It explains how registration, finance, and teaching workflows stay connected in one system. Students should identify the major modules and the data each module shares.

title: Lecture 2
text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. CSC101 Enterprise Systems lecture two focuses on workflow mapping. The note compares student registration, lecturer uploads, assessment records, and announcements. Learners should follow each workflow from data entry to reporting.

title: Lecture 3
text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. CSC101 Enterprise Systems lecture three covers dashboards, reports, and service support. It shows how indexed lecture notes improve quiz generation and chatbot guidance. Students should summarize how academic support data is presented to end users.

title: Lecture 1
text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. BIT220 Database Design lecture one introduces entities, attributes, and relationships. It uses university records such as students, courses, fees, and results as examples. Students should practice separating transactional data from reference data.

title: Lecture 2
text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. BIT220 Database Design lecture two explains first, second, and third normal form. The examples use registration tables and lecturer resource indexes. Learners should identify duplicated fields and redesign them into cleaner structures.

title: Lecture 3
text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. BIT220 Database Design lecture three demonstrates queries for results, announcements, and timetable views. It also links database design to search and retrieval support in course systems. Students should write sample queries that answer common academic questions.

title: Lecture 1
text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. BIS210 Systems Analysis lecture one introduces systems analysis in an academic portal setting. It reviews actors, goals, inputs, and outputs for students, lecturers, and administrators. Students should identify the core business processes supported by the portal.

title: Lecture 2
text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. BIS210 Systems Analysis lecture two captures requirements through interviews, observations, and use cases. The lecture note models login, resource access, translation support, and lecturer feedback. Learners should turn user goals into clear functional requirements.

title: Lecture 3
text: Year 3 Semester II 2025/2026. BIS210 Systems Analysis lecture three focuses on process flows, exceptions, and user journeys. It explains how a student opens a course unit, reads lecture PDFs, and asks for help from the chatbot. Students should diagram the flow from course selection to resource review.
